senses.definition: a person who promotes or introduces a new idea, product, or plan; someone who presents or launches something publicly
examples: The company appointed a skilled broacher to introduce the new model at the trade show.
As a persuasive broacher, she helped popularize the campaign.

senses.definition: plural of broacher; multiple people who promote or introduce new ideas or products
examples: The conference featured several broachers from different industries.
Two influential broachers spoke about the initiative.

senses.definition: to bring up a topic for discussion; to start to talk about something, often for the first time
examples: She broached the subject of financing at the meeting.
They broached the idea of a partnership during lunch.

senses.definition: a decorative or ceremonial element or a type of object used to pierce through something
examples: The broach on her jacket featured a small pearl.
A broach of the ceremonial sword was laid on the table.

senses.definition: the act of bringing up a topic for discussion; the act of starting to talk about something
examples: Broaching delicate topics requires tact.
They were broaching the issue of budget cuts.

senses.definition: having a large distance from side to side; wide
examples: The road became broad after the turn.
She has broad shoulders.

senses.definition: covering a wide range; general rather than precise
examples: Broad knowledge is useful in many jobs.
They gave a broad outline of the plan.

senses.definition: large areas of farming land used for crops; vast agricultural fields
examples: They own a broadacre farm with many fields.
Broadacre farming requires machinery and planning.

senses.definition: a broad-axe with a sharp blade used for chopping wood
examples: The lumberjack used a broadax to split logs.
He favored a heavy broadax for thick timber.

senses.definition: a large axe with a broad blade used for felling or shaping wood
examples: The old workshop contained a sturdy broadaxe.
He carved with a broad broadaxe.

senses.definition: plural of broadaxe; multiple large axes with broad blades
examples: The collection included several historic broadaxes.
He displayed two broadaxes in the shop window.

senses.definition: a high-capacity internet connection that can handle many devices at once
examples: Most homes now rely on broadband for streaming.
The school upgraded to fiber broadband.

senses.definition: plural of broadband; multiple high-capacity internet connections
examples: The university installed several broadbandbands to support labs.
Different providers offer different broadbandbands.

senses.definition: a large bean from the broad bean plant
examples: Broad beans are often eaten boiled or steamed.
She added broad beans to the salad.

senses.definition: plural of broad bean
examples: Fresh broadbeans are in season now.
They boiled the broadbeans for the stew.

senses.definition: a bird with a broad bill; also a term for broad-faced currency or a type of negotiable instrument or a high-priced bill (less common)
examples: The broadbill can be seen near the river.
The term broadbill is sometimes used in historical texts.
synonyms: other bird names depending on region

senses.definition: plural of broadbill; multiple broadbill birds
examples: Several broadbills flocked by the water.
The guide pointed out two broadbills among the trees.

senses.definition: a wide-brimmed hat or part of a hat
examples: He wore a broadbrim hat to shield the sun.
The costume included a broadbrim hat.

senses.definition: plural of broadbrim; multiple broadbrim hats
examples: Several broadbrims hung on the rack.
He bought two broadbrims for the trip.

senses.definition: a brush with a broad, flat head used for painting large areas
examples: She applied the color with a broadbrush.
A broadbrush was perfect for the background.

senses.definition: a public transmission of a program or information by radio or television
examples: The broadcast started at eight.
We watched the broadcast of the game.

senses.definition: to send out or transmit a program or information by radio or television
examples: The station will broadcast the news at 9.
They broadcast the ceremony worldwide.

senses.definition: past tense of broadcast; transmitted a program or information
examples: The message was broadcasted across the network.
The event was broadcasted live yesterday.

senses.definition: a person or organization that broadcasts programs or signals
examples: The broadcaster features news from around the world.
She works as a broadcaster for a regional TV station.

senses.definition: plural of broadcaster; multiple organizations or people that broadcast
examples: Several broadcasters covered the event.
The country has many broadcasters offering diverse programming.

senses.definition: the activity of sending out programs by radio or television; the industry of broadcasting
examples: Broadcasting is changing with new online platforms.
She studied broadcasting in college.

senses.definition: present participle of broadcast; transmitting programs
examples: The station is broadcasting now.
They are broadcasting the match live.

senses.definition: plural of broadcasting; multiple broadcasting events or programs
examples: The conference featured several broadcastings from different studios.
Viewer numbers rose across the broadcastings.

senses.definition: plural of broadcast; programs or transmissions
examples: The broadcasts last for an hour each.
They compared different broadcasts of the same event.

senses.definition: third person singular present of broadcast; transmits programs
examples: The station broadcasts daily.
They broadcast the ceremony worldwide.
